ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Reserve
Off Calendar Jun-15-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Reserve
Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Continued To Jun-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that they have not received tax clearance letters, that they have to wait three years and that the statute runs through April of 2016. Status on the reserve was continued to June 15, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Reserve
Off Calendar Sep-22-2014 Continued To Oct-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Balance Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Administrator. Douglas Voorsanger for First Church of Christ Scientist (Beneficiary).

Mr. Ferdon explained the reasons why the Public Administrator maintains a reserve and how they arrive at the amounts. Mr. Voorsanger objects to the $25,000.00 reserve amount. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Watts.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-28-2012 Continued To Mar-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Enter Orders In Accordance With Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-08-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Estate Research Associates, Inc. Douglas Voorsanger for The First Church of Christ, Scientist, in Boston, Massachusetts (Claimant). Jonathan Ferdon for Administrator of the Estate.

Mr. Connors submitted three proposed orders. One for the petition for entitlement, one for approval of the settlement agreement (copy of the settlement attached) and one that combines the two petitions. Mr. Voorsanger stated all three Counsel participated in preparing the orders. Counsel advised the Court of some of the changes they made to the orders. The matters were taken under submission for review of the proposed orders.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Rob Harris.

Petition For A Court Determination Of The Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Decedents Estate
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Jan-05-2011 Continued To Mar-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For A Court Determination Of The Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Decedents Estate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2010 Continued To Jan-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Douglas Voorsanger for The First Church of Scientist in Boston Massuchettes. Brian Connors for Eatate Research Associates, Inc. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr. Connors filed the assignment and a family tree, that he did not have the opportunity to review the documents completely, that they were documents to support Mr. Connnors' Clients' positions and that he believed Mr. Connors Clients do have standing. Mr. Connors suggested putting the matter over for Mr. Voorsanger's Clients to determine if they want to engage in settlement discussions. The matter was continued to January 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of settlement discussions.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.

Petition For A Court Determination Of The Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Decedents Estate
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Continued To Oct-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Douglas Voorsanger and William Holsman for The First Church of Christ, Scientist, in Boston, Massachusetts. Brian Connors for Estate Research Associates, Inc. Jonathan Ferdon for Administrator.

Mr. Connors stated that they have been talking about how to approach the matter, and that they needed time for discovery to determine if there are any further facts and then have a briefing shedule. Mr. Voorsanger stated that he would like to see Mr. Connors' theories and raised the issue of Mr. Connors' Client having standing. Mr. Ferdon stated that there was no support from the Claimants, that Estate Research has not documented the family tree at all, that there were no birth certificates or death certificates and that they don't have assignments of Estate Research Associates. Mr. Connors stated that he was prepared to submit all documents necessary to establish heirship, the assignments from the Heirs to Estate Research and raised the issue of The First Church of Christ having standing. Said documents are to be filed by September 28, 2010. The matter was continued to October 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of standing of Mr. Connors' Clients and discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
